schemaStatus.cloudPersistence: The full DATA object is embedded in simurg_data.payload.data under the fixed id main.
schemaStatus.backup: JSON export serializes the full DATA object; general JSON restore replaces DATA wholesale.
schemaStatus.notes: Fields are created lazily by different modules. Shape descriptions below reflect current code, not an enforced contract.

stores.name: workouts
mutatedAt: Workout Logger edit/add/delete functions in index.html
persistedAt: Push To Cloud -> full DATA payload
stores.duplicateBehavior: Workout import uses a composite comparison of date, exercise, startTime, sets, reps and weight. Manual additions and general restore have no global uniqueness constraint.
stores.modelWarning: Current seed and Gym editor use one row per individual set with sets=1. Some aggregators count rows as sets; SimurgSignalModel and workout-source-policy multiply reps/volume by row.sets. An imported row with sets>1 is therefore treated differently across calculations.

stores.name: metrics
stores.duplicateRisk: high; repeated dates are appended without deduplication

stores.name: nutrition
stores.duplicateRisk: high; repeated dates are appended without deduplication

stores.name: recovery
stores.duplicateRisk: high; repeated dates are appended without deduplication
stores.notes: SimurgSignalModel uses this as a fallback behind Polar Nightly Recharge and recoveryEntries.

stores.name: appleWatch
stores.duplicateRisk: medium; current importer compares date, startTime and duration, but missing time/duration can collapse distinct records or permit semantically duplicated aliases

stores.name: dailyNotes
stores.duplicateRisk: high; daily records are appended with no date uniqueness rule

stores.name: weeklyNotes
stores.duplicateRisk: high; no week key or uniqueness rule is enforced

stores.name: customGymPrograms
stores.shape: Object<dateOrProgramKey, {overrides: Object<exerciseName, {name, bodyPart, setCount}>, extras: Array<{id, name, bodyPart, setCount, custom}>}>
createdAt: lazy initialization in Gym code and pull/import defaults
stores.stableRecordIds: partial; extras receive timestamp-derived IDs, overrides are keyed by mutable exercise name

stores.name: programNames
createdAt: lazy initialization in program-name editor and pull/import defaults
stores.stableRecordIds: not applicable; values are keyed by day name
stores.duplicateRisk: low, but arbitrary imported keys/values are accepted

stores.name: polarWorkouts
stores.stableRecordIds: partial; polarExerciseId exists, but frontend deduplicates primarily by date plus startTime
stores.duplicateRisk: medium; missing/equal startTime can merge distinct sessions, while changed startTime can duplicate the same Polar exercise

stores.name: polarActivity
stores.stableRecordIds: polarActivityId is retained, but daily storage is keyed by date
stores.duplicateRisk: low for one daily summary; same-date updates overwrite/merge

stores.name: polarSleep
stores.shape: {daily: Object<YYYY-MM-DD, PolarSleep>, latest: PolarSleep|null, lastSyncAt: string|null, lastError: string|null}
stores.recordFields: date, source, start/end, duration, sleep score, continuity, cycles, interruptions, deep/light/REM/awake stages, sleep goal/charge, sanitized raw payload

stores.name: polarNightlyRecharge
stores.recordFields: date, source, nightly HR, beat-to-beat, HRV, breathing rate, recharge/ANS status, sample collections, sanitized raw payload

stores.name: polarContinuousHr
stores.stableRecordIds: date key only; samples have no explicit IDs
stores.duplicateRisk: low at day level, undefined at sample level
stores.recordFields: date, source, samples[{sampleTime, heartRate}], sampleCount, average/min/max HR, sanitized raw payload

stores.name: polarCardioLoad
stores.recordFields: date, source, cardio load/status, strain, tolerance, ratio, level, sanitized raw payload

stores.name: polarConnection
stores.shape: {connected, status, lastSyncAt, lastError, source, polarUserId?, connectedAt?, tokenExpiresAt?, lastCounts?, lastStatuses?, lastCategoryErrors?}
stores.stableRecordIds: contains a Polar user identifier but no Simurg user/device ownership ID
stores.securityNote: Access and refresh tokens are not stored here; they remain encrypted in server-side Polar tables.

additionalLazyStores.name: _meta
additionalLazyStores.notes: This is build metadata, not an enforced DATA schema version.

crossCuttingRisks: No authenticated owner or user_id is represented in DATA.
No top-level schema_version or migration pipeline exists.
Most records lack stable immutable IDs.
General restore and cloud pull replace DATA without schema validation.
Full-object writes increase conflict and accidental data-loss impact.
The workouts sets field is interpreted inconsistently between row-count and multiplier calculations.
